L1.area: External service configuration and connectivity management

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled configuration values for an external service's process-based transport are stored in the database without sanitization and later passed directly to a subprocess execution call, allowing authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

L3.area: MCP service stdio transport configuration and connection testing logic
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled command and argument values in MCP stdio transport configuration are stored without validation and later used directly in subprocess execution when testing the service connection, allowing authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

ground_truth.reason: The MCP service creation and update endpoints accept user-controlled stdio_config.command and args values without validation, which are then passed directly to subprocess execution when testing the service connection, enabling remote code execution.
